title: 第 31 節
en: 4. 'When I said, I turn to Pra;za, then Pra/za means all whatever exists here — to that I turn.'
5. ' When I said, I turn to Bhu/z, what I said is, I turn to the earth, the sky, and heaven.'
1 The object of this section, the Kojavi^ilana, is to show how the promise made in III, 13, 6, 'that a strong son should be born in a man's family,' is to be fulfilled.

2 These names are explained by the commentator as follows : Because people offer libations (^uhvati), turning to the east, therefore it is called Guhu. Because evil doers suffer (sahante) in the town of Yama, which is in the south, therefore it is called Sahamana. The western quarter is called Ra^-fii, either because it is sacred to king Varuwa (ra^-an), or on account of the red colour (raga) of the twilight. The north is called Subhuta, because wealthy beings (bhutimat), like Kuvera &c, reside there.
3 Here the names of the sons are to be pronounced.
[3] E 50 A'tfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
6. ' When I said, I turn to Bhuva^, what I said A is, I turn to Agni (fire), Vayu (air), Aditya (sun).'
7. ' When I said, I turn to Sva/^, what I said is, I turn to the J&g-veda, Ya^ur-veda, and Sama-veda. That is what I said, yea, that is what I said/ Sixteenth Khanda1.

1. Man is sacrifice. His (first) twenty-four years are the morning-libation. The Gayatri has twentyfour syllables, the morning-libation is offered with Gayatri hymns. The Vasus are connected with that part of the sacrifice. The Pra^as (the five senses) are the Vasus, for they make all this to abide (vasayanti).

2. If anything ails him in that (early) age, let him say: ' Ye Pra^as, ye Vasus, extend this my morninglibation unto the midday-libation, that I, the sacrificer, may not perish in the midst of the Pra;zas or Vasus.' Thus he recovers from his illness, and becomes whole.

3. The next forty-four years are the middaylibation. The Trish/ubh has forty-four syllables, the midday-libation is offered with TrishAibh hymns. The Rudras are connected with that part of it. The Pra^as are the Rudras, for they make all this to cry (rodayanti).

4. If anything ails him in that (second) age, let him say : ' Ye Pra^as, ye Rudras, extend this my midday-libation unto the third libation, that I, the sacrificer, may not perish in the midst of the Pra^as or Rudras.' Thus he recovers from his illness, and becomes whole.
5. The next forty-eight years are the third 1 The object of this Khanda, is to show how to obtain long life, as promised before.

Ill PRAPATFAKA, I 7 KHAiVDA, 5. 5 1 libation. The c7agati has forty-eight syllables, the third libation is offered with Ga.ga.ti hymns. The Adityas are connected with that part of it. The A Pra/^as are the Adityas, for they take up all this (adadate).

6. If anything ails him in that (third) age, let him say : ' Ye Pra^as, ye Adityas, extend this my third libation unto the full age, that I, the sacrificer, may not perish in the midst of the Pra;/as or Adityas.' Thus he recovers from his illness, and becomes whole.

7. Mahidasa Aitareya (the son of Itara), who knew this, said (addressing a disease) : ' Why dost thou afflict me, as I shall not die by it ?' He lived a hundred and sixteen years (i.e. 24 + 44 + 48). He, too, who knows this lives on to a hundred and sixteen years.
Seventeenth Khajvda1.
1. When a man (who is the sacrificer) hungers, thirsts, and abstains from pleasures, that is the Diksha (initiatory rite).
2. When a man eats, drinks, and enjoys pleasures, he does it with the Upasadas (the sacrificial days on which the sacrificer is allowed to partake of food).
3. When a man laughs, eats, and delights himself, he does it with the Stuta-^astras (hymns sung and recited at the sacrifices).
4. Penance, liberality, righteousness, kindness, truthfulness, these form his Dakshmas (gifts bestowed on priests, &c.)

5. Therefore when they say, ' There will be a 1 Here we have a representation of the sacrifice as performed without any ceremonial, and as it is often represented when performed in thought only by a man living in the forest.
E 2 52 JTffANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.

birth,' and ' there has been a birth ' (words used at the Soma-sacrifice, and really meaning, ' He will pour out the Soma-juice,' and ' he has poured out the Soma-juice'), that is his new birth. His death is the Avabhrztha ceremony (when the sacrificial vessels are carried away to be cleansed).

6. Ghora Angirasa, after having communicated this (view of the sacrifice) to Krishna., the son of Devaki ] — and he never thirsted again (after other knowledge) — said : ' Let a man, when his end ap- 1 The curious coincidence between Krishna. Devakiputra, here mentioned as a pupil of Ghora Ahgirasa, and the famous Krz'shwa, the son of Devaki, was first pointed out by Colebrooke, Miscell. Essays, II, 177. Whether it is more than a coincidence, is difficult to say. Certainly we can build no other conclusions on it than those indicated by Colebrooke, that new fables may have been constructed elevating this personage to the rank of a god. We know absolutely nothing of the old Krishna. Devakiputra except his having been a pupil of Ghora Ahgirasa, nor does there seem to have been any attempt made by later Brahmans to connect their divine Krishna., the son of Vasudeva, with the Krishna. Devakiputra of our Upanishad. This is all the more remarkable because the author of the »Sa«</ilya-sutras, for instance, who is very anxious to find a jrauta authority for the worship of Kn'shsa Vasudeva as the supreme deity, had to be satisfied with quoting such modern compilations as the Naraya/zopanishad, Atharva^iras, VI, 9, brahma;zyo devakiputro brahma«yo madhusudana/z (see Sandilya-sutras, ed. Ballantyne, p. 36, translated by Cowell, p. 51), without venturing to refer to the Krishna. Devakiputra of the A^Mndogya-upanishad. The occurrence of such names as Krz'sh?za, Vasudeva, Madhusudana stamps Upanishads, like the Atmabodhaupanishad, as modern (Colebrooke, Essays, I, 10 1), and the same remark applies, as Weber has shown, to the Gopalatapani-upanishad (Bibliotheca Indica, No. 183), where we actually find such names as Snkrishna Govinda, Gopi^anavallabha, Devakyam gatah (p. 38), &c. Professor Weber has treated these questions very fully, but it is not quite clear to me whether he wishes to go beyond Colebrooke and to admit more than a similarity of name between the pupil of Ghora Ahgirasa and the friend of the Gopis.

Ill PRAPAFtfAKA, 1 8 KHANDA, 2. 53 proaches, take refuge with this Triad * : " Thou art the imperishable," "Thou art the unchangeable," ' Thou art the edge of Pra^a." ' On this subject there are two Rik verses (Rig-veda VIII, 6, 30): —
7. ' Then they see (within themselves) the everpresent light of the old seed (of the world, the Sat), the highest, which is lighted in the brilliant (Brahman).' Rig-veda I, 50, 10: —

' Perceiving above the darkness (of ignorance) the higher light (in the sun), as the higher light within the heart, the bright source (of light and life) among the gods, we have reached the highest light, yea, the highest light2.'
Eighteenth Khaa^da 3.

1. Let a man meditate on mind as Brahman (n.), this is said with reference to the body. Let a man meditate on the ether as Brahman (n.), this is said with reference to the Devas. Thus both the meditation which has reference to the body, and the meditation which has reference to the Devas, has been taught.
zh: 4. 「當我說：我轉向普拉吒那（Pra*z*a）時，普拉吒那就意味著凡是在此處存在的一切——我轉向那個。」
5. 『當我說：我轉向 Bhūḥ，我所說的是：我轉向地、空與天。』
本章節的對象——《科賈維羅那》（Kojavi^ilana）——其目的在於說明第三卷第十三章第六節中所作的承諾，即「一個強壯的兒子將誕生於某人的家族中」，將如何得以實現。

2 註釋者對這些名稱的解釋如下：因為人們向東轉身獻祭奠酒（*uhvati*），所以它被稱為古虎（Guhu）。因為惡行者在南方閻摩（Yama）的城鎮中受苦（*sahante*），所以它被稱為薩哈瑪那（Sahamana）。西方被稱為羅提（Ra·fii），要麼是因為它奉獻於國王伐樓那（*ra·an*），要麼是因為暮光的紅色（*raga*）。北方被稱為蘇布塔（Subhuta），因為財富豐盛的眾生（*bhutimat*），如俱毘羅（Kuvera）等，住在那裡。
3 於此處，諸子之名應當被誦念。
[3] E 50 歌者奧義書（Aitareya-Upanishad）。
6. 「當我說『我轉向婆羅』時，我所說的 A 是『我轉向阿格尼（火）、伐由（風）、阿迪提亞（太陽）』。」
「當我說『我轉向自我』時，我所說的是，我轉向梨俱吠陀、夜柔吠陀與娑摩吠陀。這就是我所說的，誠然，這就是我所說的。」第十六章。
人是祭祀。他的（首）二十四年是晨間奠酒。《迦耶特里》（Gayatri）有二十四個音節，晨間奠酒是以迦耶特里讚歌獻祭的。瓦蘇神祇（the Vasus）與祭祀的該部分相關。普拉那斯（Pra^as，五種感官）就是瓦蘇神祇，因為它們使一切得以安住（vasayanti）。
2. 若在那（早期的）時代有任何疾病纏身，讓他說：『汝等普拉那斯（Pra^as），汝等瓦蘇斯（Vasus），願汝等將我此晨間奠祭延伸至正午奠祭，使我祭祀者不致於喪身於普拉那斯或瓦蘇斯之中。』如此他便從疾病中恢復，身體復原。
3. 接下來的四十四年乃是正午奠酒之時。梨俱吠陀三行詩（Trisṭubh）具有四十四個音節，正午奠酒乃以梨俱吠陀三行詩頌歌獻祭。魯陀羅神祇與其中此部分相關。布拉那斯乃是魯陀羅神祇，蓋因彼等致使此一切而號啕大哭（rodayanti）。
4. 若他在那（第二）個時代患有任何疾病，應當如此言說：『汝等風神（Pra^as），汝等魯陀羅神（Rudras），願汝等延伸此吾之正午祭酒至第三次祭酒，使吾祭祀者不至於在風神或魯陀羅神之中喪亡。』如此，他便從疾病中恢復，並得以全身。
5. 接下來的四十八年是第三個時期。¹ 這個 Khanda 的目的是說明如何獲得長壽，如前所許諾的那樣。 ¹ 本 Khanda 的目的在於闡示如何獲得長壽，如前所承諾者。
第三祭祀。迦迦蒂韻文有四十八個音節，第三次奠祭是以迦迦蒂讚歌獻祭的。阿迪提耶神族與其相關部分相連。阿普拉那是阿迪提耶神族，因為它們承載了一切（adadate）。
6. 若彼人在那第三個時期有所苦患，當言曰：『汝等普拉那斯，汝等阿迪提耶斯，願汝等延展我此第三次奠祭至圓滿之齡，俾我祭祀者勿於普拉那斯或阿迪提耶斯中喪命。』如是則彼人得免其疾，復歸完整。
馬希達薩‧艾達睿耶（Mahidasa Aitareya，依塔羅之子），知曉此事者，說道（對著疾病說）：「汝何以折磨我，我終不會因此而死？」他活了一百一十六歲（即24 + 44 + 48）。凡知此者，亦將活至一百一十六歲。
第十七梨俱吠陀
當一個人（即祭祀者）感到飢餓、口渴，並克制享樂時，那就是 Diksha（入教儀式）。
2. 當一個人進食、飲水並享受快樂時，他是以烏帕薩達（Upasadas）（祭祀者被允許享用食物的祭祀日）來進行的。
3. 當一個人歡笑、進食並使自己喜悅時，他是藉由《誦經本集》（Stuta-śāstras，在祭祀時吟唱和誦讀的讚歌）來進行這些行為的。
4. 苦行、慷慨、正義、仁善、誠實，這些構成了他的達克什瑪斯（Dakshmas）（贈予祭司等人的禮物）。
5. 因此當他們說道，「將會有¹」時，我們在此看到犧牲儀式的一種表現，其形式不帶任何儀典色彩，而且正如經常所見，當一個住在森林中的人僅在心念中進行這種儀式時的表現方式。 ¹ 這裡我們看到犧牲儀式的一種表現，乃是不涉任何儀式而進行，而且往往就如一個住在森林中的人單純在思想中進行時所代表的樣子。
「誕生」與「已有誕生」（蘇摩祭儀中使用的詞語，實則意為「他將倒出蘇摩汁液」與「他已倒出蘇摩汁液」），這就是他的新生。他的死亡乃是阿跋赫瑞塔儀式（Avabhrztha ceremony），即祭祀器皿被帶去淨潔之時。

6. 高羅‧安吉拉薩（Ghora Angirasa）將此（關於祭祀的見解）傳授給德瓦基之子克里希那（Krishna, the son of Devaki），他從此不再渴求其他知識——之後說：「一個人，當他生命將盡時，¹ ¹ 克里希那‧德瓦基普特拉（Krishna. Devakiputra）與著名的克里希那（Krz'shwa），德瓦基之子，兩者之間的奇異巧合，首次由科爾布魯克指出，見《雜論集》第二卷第177頁。這是否不止於巧合，很難說。確實我們只能得出科爾布魯克所指示的結論，即新的傳說可能被編造出來，將此人物提升至神的地位。除了曾是高羅‧安吉拉薩之弟子這一事實外，我們對古老的克里希那‧德瓦基普特拉一無所知；後世的婆羅門似乎也沒有嘗試將他們的神聖克里希那（克里希那，瓦蘇德瓦之子）與我們《奧義書》中的克里希那‧德瓦基普特拉相連結。這尤為值得注意，因為《三卡耶經》（Saṃkārya-sutras）的作者例如甚為渴望為克里希那‧瓦蘇德瓦（Kn'shsa Vasudeva）作為最高神祇的崇拜尋得《韋達》的權威，卻只能滿足於引用《那羅延奧義書》（Naraya/zopanishad）、《雅圖瓦韋達》第6卷第9節等較為現代的彙編文獻：「婆羅門是德瓦基之子，婆羅門是摩度蘇達那」（見《三卡耶經》，巴拉坦因版，第36頁，考維爾譯本，第51頁），而不敢引用《阿特瓦爾達奧義書》中的克里希那‧德瓦基普特拉。克里希那、瓦蘇德瓦、摩度蘇達那等名字的出現，使得《大我知識奧義書》（Atmabodhaupanishad）等《奧義書》標上了現代的印記（科爾布魯克，《論文集》第一卷第101頁），同樣的評論也適用於《牧童塔帕尼奧義書》（Gopalatapani-upanishad），如韋伯所示（《印度文獻》第183號），其中我們實際上發現了如「施里克里希那‧戈文達」（Snkrishna Govinda）、「牧女的寵兒」（Gopi^anavallabha）、「生於德瓦基」（Devakyam gatah）等名字（第38頁），等等。韋伯教授對這些問題進行了相當深入的論述，但我對於他是否想要超越科爾布魯克、承認高羅‧安吉拉薩的弟子與牧女們的友人之間不止於名字的相似性，卻不甚明確。
《吠陀梵書》(Ill Prapattaka, 1 Khanda, 2. 53)記載，當死亡逼近之時，應當向此三位一體尋求庇護：「汝為不朽者」、「汝為不變者」、「汝為般若（Pra^a）之邊緣」。關於此事，《梨俱吠陀》(Rig-veda VIII, 6, 30)有兩首讚頌詩：
7. 「然後他們看見（在他們自身之內）那永恆存在的古老種子（世界的，薩特〔Sat〕）之光，最高者，它被點燃於輝煌的（梵天〔Brahman〕）。」《梨俱吠陀》第一卷，第五十讚歌，第十頌 —
'我們在黑暗（無知）之上，感知到那更高的光（在太陽中），及心中所有的更高之光，眾神之中那光明的源頭（光與生命之源），我們由此達致最高的光，誠然，最高的光²。'
第十八卡摩陀第三章
讓一個人以心靈冥想梵天（n.），據說這是相對於身體而言的。讓一個人以以太冥想梵天（n.），據說這是相對於提婆而言的。因此，無論是相對於身體的冥想，還是相對於提婆的冥想，都已被闡述了。
